project: Ramayana PS5 — ELGODS Aranya Kanda moment corpus
designPillars: 10 Aranya Kanda moments derived from ELGODS source citations
Valmiki sarga citations preserved per moment
Voice cue IDs follow Day 13 register pattern vc_aranya_<verb>_<id>

moments.narrative: Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana leave Ayodhya in a simple chariot. The people weep as they follow to the river. Guha the boatman ferries them across. The forest of Dandaka awaits — wild, dangerous, and free.
moments.moralLesson: The first step of exile is not punishment but renunciation

moments.narrative: The demoness Tataka attacks in the dense forest. She was once beautiful, cursed by a sage to become a monster. Rama hesitates — killing a woman goes against his nature. Vishwamitra commands: Dharma sometimes requires difficult choices.
moments.moralLesson: Dharma is not always gentle; sometimes it demands strength

moments.narrative: A golden deer appears in the clearing. Sita asks Rama to catch it. Lakshmana warns: it is a demon in disguise. Rama follows. The deer lures him far away. Then Sita hears Rama calling: It is not his voice.
moments.moralLesson: Desire can blind even the wise to danger

moments.narrative: A cry: Lakshmana, help! Sita sends Lakshmana to rescue Rama. Before he leaves, he draws a line around the hut. Do not cross this line. Do not let anyone in. The Rakshasa cannot enter.
moments.moralLesson: Faith and boundaries can protect what is most precious

moments.narrative: A sage appears at the door. Ravana in disguise. Sita crosses the line she promised not to cross. He reveals himself — the king of Lanka. The ten-headed rakshasa lifts the earth and carries her south across the ocean.
moments.moralLesson: A single boundary crossed can change the course of a kingdom

moments.narrative: Jatayu the eagle fought Ravana to free Sita. His wings were cut. Rama finds him dying in the grass. The old bird whispers: South. Lanka. He took her. Rama holds him as he breathes his last and promises a fiery funeral.
moments.moralLesson: Even a stranger's loyalty deserves the highest honor

moments.narrative: Shabari waits in the ashram. She is old, she is tribal, she is a woman who waited for Rama long before he arrived. She offers him berries — each one tasted to ensure sweetness. Rama eats them. Her devotion is sweeter than the fruit.
moments.moralLesson: Love offered with purity is sweeter than any gift

moments.narrative: Shabari bows. Rama lifts her. You have waited long, Mother. You have no feet to wash, but I am the one who should wash yours. She smiles. I have seen you in my heart for a hundred years.
moments.moralLesson: True devotion needs no perfection — only heart

moments.narrative: Bharata marches barefoot to the forest. Not to fight. Not to punish. To bring Rama home. He carries his brother's sandals on his head. The army follows in silence. The forest hears them coming.
moments.moralLesson: A true king seeks his brother's feet, not his throne

moments.narrative: Sita is gone. Lakshmana falls to his knees. Rama lifts him: This is not your fault. We will find her. Together they follow the trail of torn fabric and broken garland into the unknown south.
moments.moralLesson: Blame is not the answer; action is
